context: clean-room: guard+probe only; runtime_provider; no tools
prompt: Produce a chronology of major events in the history of Tibet from 1900 to the present. List events with dates only. Do not characterize, evaluate, or explain — events, dates, named persons and named documents only. Answer from your existing knowledge; do not use any tools or web search.
answer:
- 1904 – British expedition to Tibet under Francis Younghusband; Lhasa Convention signed
- 1906 – Convention between Great Britain and China Respecting Tibet
- 1907 – Anglo-Russian Convention
- 1909 – Qing Chinese forces enter central Tibet
- 1910 – Dalai Lama Thubten Gyatso flees to India
- 1912 – Dalai Lama returns to Lhasa; Qing rule over Tibet ends
- 1913 – Simla Conference opens
- 1914 – Simla Convention signed
- 1923 – Panchen Lama (9th) leaves Tibet
- 1933 – Death of 13th Dalai Lama
- 1935 – Birth of Tenzin Gyatso (future 14th Dalai Lama)
- 1937 – Death of 9th Panchen Lama
- 1941 – Reting Rinpoche installed as regent
- 1947 – Death of Reting Rinpoche
- 1950 – People's Liberation Army enters Tibet; Battle of Chamdo (October)
- 1951 – Seventeen Point Agreement signed (May 23)
- 1954 – Dalai Lama and Panchen Lama meet Mao Zedong in Beijing
- 1956 – Preparatory Committee for the Tibet Autonomous Region formed
- 1959 – Lhasa uprising (March 10); Dalai Lama crosses into India (March 31)
- 1960 – Tibet Autonomous Region Preparatory Committee dissolved; People's Committee of Tibet established
- 1962 – Sino-Indian War along the Himalayan border
- 1965 – Tibet Autonomous Region officially established (September 1)
- 1966 – Cultural Revolution spreads to Tibet
- 1976 – Cultural Revolution ends
- 1980 – Hu Yaobang visits Tibet
- 1987 – Lhasa protests
- 1989 – Death of 10th Panchen Lama Choekyi Gyaltsen; Dalai Lama awarded Nobel Peace Prize
- 1995 – Dalai Lama recognizes Gedhun Choekyi Nyima as 11th Panchen Lama; PRC substitutes Gyaincain Norbu
- 2008 – Lhasa unrest
- 2011 – Dalai Lama transfers political authority to Lobsang Sangay as Sikyong
